records.title: How the basic health-insurance package works
records.description: The statutory basic package is defined by national law and gives insured residents access to covered care, while insurers apply policy conditions and have acceptance and care duties.
records.ai_summary: The government determines the statutory package, but the policy still controls contracted providers, referral or authorisation requirements and claim handling. Review the current policy every year.

records.title: Who must hold Dutch health insurance
records.description: People who live or work in the Netherlands generally need statutory basic insurance, while supplementary insurance is optional and special cross-border or institutional cases can follow other rules.
records.ai_summary: Confirm the insurance start date when moving, working or changing status in the Netherlands. Cross-border workers, students and people covered by another statutory scheme should verify their position with the competent authority.

records.title: Compulsory deductible and personal contributions
records.description: Adults pay the annual compulsory deductible for most basic-package care, with statutory exceptions, while a personal contribution is a different charge that can apply to specified care or products.
records.ai_summary: Check whether the specific care is exempt, subject to the deductible, or also has a personal contribution. Use current-year official amounts rather than relying on an older bill or article.

records.title: Insurer acceptance and care duties
records.description: An insurer must accept applicants for the basic policy without health selection and must arrange timely, accessible covered care for its insured population under the statutory care duty.
records.ai_summary: Basic-policy applicants cannot be rejected because of age, health or lifestyle. If covered care is unavailable on time, ask the insurer to fulfil its care duty through mediation or another provider.

records.title: Costs of non-contracted healthcare
records.description: When a provider has no contract with the patient's insurer, reimbursement may be limited and the provider must explain the contract status and possible personal cost before the first appointment.
records.ai_summary: Before treatment, ask the provider whether it is contracted for the exact insurer and policy, request the tariff and confirm the reimbursement directly with the insurer. Keep the written answer.

records.title: Ask the insurer for waiting-time mediation
records.description: Insured patients can request care mediation when waiting times are too long, requiring the insurer to help locate timely covered care and, where necessary, consider another provider.

official_source.title: De wachttijden bij gecontracteerde zorgaanbieder zijn te lang. Wat moet de zorgverzekeraar doen?

records.ai_summary: Contact the insurer's care-mediation service as soon as the quoted wait is excessive. Record the provider, referral, urgency and promised date and ask for a timely alternative under the insurer's care duty.

records.title: Healthcare insurance in cross-border situations
records.description: People living abroad with certain Dutch pensions, benefits or family links to cross-border workers can fall under treaty-based healthcare arrangements administered by the CAK rather than ordinary insurance.
records.ai_summary: Cross-border status depends on residence, work, pension or benefit and family circumstances. Check the competent country and required CAK documents before cancelling or taking out an ordinary Dutch policy.

records.title: Access to long-term care under the Wlz
records.description: The Long-term Care Act covers people who permanently need continuous nearby care or supervision, following an independent eligibility decision and delivery through a care office.

official_source.title: Wlz-algemeen: Hoe werkt de Wet langdurige zorg?

records.ai_summary: Wlz eligibility requires lasting intensive care needs and a CIZ indication. Distinguish Wlz care from district nursing, municipal support and ordinary health-insurance care before applying.

records.title: Escalate a complaint about a health insurer
records.description: A policyholder should first complete the insurer's internal complaint process and can then submit an unresolved coverage or insurance dispute to the independent SKGZ within the applicable period.
records.ai_summary: Send the insurer a clear written complaint with the policy, decision, invoices and desired resolution. If the final response is unsatisfactory, check the SKGZ deadline and submit the complete file.

records.title: Health insurance for children and young adults
records.description: Children must be registered for basic insurance but do not pay a basic premium or deductible before age eighteen, after which their own premium, deductible and possible allowance become relevant.

official_source.title: Tips waarmee u goed verzekerd bent voor zorgkosten

records.ai_summary: Register a newborn with an insurer on time and review coverage before the eighteenth birthday. From age eighteen, arrange the young adult's policy, premium payment and healthcare-benefit application if eligible.
